recs.s: NEW & ACTIONABLE: setup_cache_memory_git.sh fails 'fatal: not in a git directory' (exit 128) after a cache hit when the restored cache dir has data files but no valid .git. Today this failed 2/2 cache-memory-enabled workflows (Chaos PR Bundle Fuzzer, Copilot PR Prompt Pattern Analysis). The same fault also fails the downstream 'Commit cache-memory changes' step. FIX: after restore, verify the repo with `git -C "$GH_AW_CACHE_DIR" rev-parse --git-dir` (or test -d .git) and, when files are present but it is not a valid git repo, `git init` + re-add the remote/branch instead of trusting the cache-hit flag and immediately running git commands. Add a regression guard for the 'non-git files present after restore' branch. This is the single highest-value fix in the queue today (one script, restores 2 workflows).

serena find_symbol/get_symbols_overview return 'MCP error 0 EOF' on first query in Smoke Claude (run 26662945808) — the Go LSP backend crashes after a successful activate_project. Action: capture serena/gopls stderr in the smoke run, check gopls version compatibility with the repo's Go toolchain, and confirm whether the LSP needs a warm-up/index step before symbol queries. Run passed overall so this is medium, but it silently removes symbol-search coverage from the Claude smoke test.

Two tool-availability gaps surfaced in smoke tests: (1) mcpscripts CLI has no `gh` passthrough (Smoke Claude expected mcpscripts-gh; only github_pr_query/issue/discussion/go/make exist) and (2) web-fetch MCP tool is absent for Smoke Codex. Both runs passed. Decide per tool: either provision the tool or update the smoke-test prompt to stop expecting it, so missing-tool noise stops being reported.

CARRIED: Avenger did not run in today's window, so the --max-turns 25 / isMaxTurnsExit issue is unverified. Keep the recommendation to set max-turns: ~50 in .github/workflows/avenger.md open and re-evaluate on the next cycle in which Avenger runs.

CARRIED (no recurrence in partial window): target=* without explicit number affected 3 tools on 05-28 but none failed today. Keep the MCP-boundary validator recommendation: reject any target=* tool call whose context cannot auto-resolve to an issue/PR, with an error naming the missing field, so the agent self-corrects instead of failing post-run. Confirm over a full-day window before closing.
RESOLVED today: Changeset Generator on gpt-5.4 succeeded after 9 days stuck. Action: confirm no recurrence over the next 2 cycles, then this issue can be fully closed. No code change needed.
REVERSED today ($11.33 -> $3.07). No action; keep watching for re-escalation of the prompt-growth pattern.
